title: Volume
summary: Specializes cutoff functionals to renormalized PE volume and its anomaly.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes cutoff functionals to renormalized PE volume and its anomaly.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/PE/Renormalization/Volume.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: PEVolume Cutoff Data. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: volume Cutoff Functional. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def volumeCutoffFunctional (X : PESpace.{u, v}) : CutoffFunctional (PEVolumeCutoffData.{u, v, w} X) Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Renormalized Volume. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def RenormalizedVolume {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Volume Anomaly. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def VolumeAnomaly {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Selects or identifies the constant term in a Laurent/log cutoff expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem RenormalizedVolume_eq_finitePart {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : RenormalizedVolume D = finitePart D.volumeExpansion

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Selects or identifies the logarithmic coefficient in a cutoff expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VolumeAnomaly_eq_logAnomaly {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : VolumeAnomaly D = logAnomaly D.volumeExpansion

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
